subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear title that identifies the exploration topic
Discernible structure with introduction, modelling, calculation, and conclusion sections
Use of headings to guide the reader
weaknesses: Transitions between sections are abrupt and lack justification for function choices
Extended narrative and repeated background reduce conciseness
Figures are uncaptioned or introduced without discussion
Inconsistent citation and heading styles in the bibliography
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report shows a basic but adequate organization and logical development (band 2). Headings and a four-part structure help the reader follow the line of thought, but abrupt transitions, missing definitions and justifications, and unnecessary narrative lower the overall coherence and conciseness. A score of 2/4 reflects these mixed strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Consistent use of standard mathematical symbols (â«, Ï, f(x))
Multiple representations including GeoGebra screenshots and plotted graphs
Explanations are generally clear and follow mathematical conventions
weaknesses: Several figures are too small or blurred and lack axis labels or unit markings
Notation for limits, differential elements (dx), and units is inconsistent or omitted
Some equations and representations are separated from their explanations, causing jumps in logic
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there are lapses in labelling and occasional unclear jumps, the student uses appropriate notation throughout, employs multiple representations effectively, and communicates ideas clearly for the most part. This aligns more closely with band 3 in Mathematical Communication, warranting 3/4.

strengths: Personal story about assisting a grandfather provides genuine motivation
Reflective commentary on personal growth and IB identity adds authenticity
weaknesses: Mathematical decisions (function types, interval breaks) are not justified from a personal perspective
Limited evidence of independent adaptation or novel approaches to the problem
No exploration of alternative strategies or error analyses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows some personal connection and a clear narrative, but independent thinking and creative initiative in the mathematical approach are minimal. The evidence corresponds to band 1, so 1/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Acknowledgement of measurement error in brief comments
Restatement of aims in the conclusion
weaknesses: Reflection is superficial and confined to general statements
No deep critique of modelling choices or numerical results
Lacks connection between findings and enhanced mathematical understanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection remains at a basic level with minimal critical analysis or insight into implications. This limited depth and critique place the work in band 1, meriting 1/3.

strengths: Correct application of the disk-method formula V=â«Ï[f(x)]Â²dx
Use of piecewise polynomial fitting and definite integration at SL course level
Engages with modelling of a real object using appropriate calculus techniques
weaknesses: Major numerical and algebraic errors lead to implausible volumes
Integration limits conflict with measured dimensions and are not justified
Misunderstanding of Riemann sums and lack of error/uncertainty analysis
Piecewise segmentation is not explained or justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant SL mathematicsâdisk integration and polynomial fittingâbut significant errors and conceptual gaps limit accuracy and rigour. Overall performance aligns between bands 2 and 3; a middle score of 3/6 reflects both the correct methodology and the serious flaws in execution.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice of object and personal story show some initiative, but the mathematical process follows textbook disk-method steps without original adaptation.  Evidence of genuine independent decision-making is limited.

The student shows some understanding of revolving solids and function fitting but explanations are brief and sometimes conceptually weak (e.g. misuse of âRiemann sumâ).  Understanding is evident yet incomplete, consistent with band 2.

Several integral results are implausible (millions of cmÂ³ for a 28 cm vessel) indicating major numerical or algebraic errors; no checks are performed.  Hence the mathematics contains âmajor errors.â

Large segments of anecdotal narrative and extended quotations add little to the mathematics.  Repetition of background statements and very long, un-interpreted integral results reduce conciseness.  Only âsome irrelevant materialâ is removed, so band 1 is most fitting.

Standard symbols (â«, Ï, f(x)) are used and variables are occasionally defined, but notation for limits, units and disk radii is inconsistent or missing.  There is some appropriate language yet frequent lapses (e.g. âinfinite is not a numberâ).  This aligns with âsome appropriate mathematical language and notation.â

Most explanations can be followed, yet sudden jumps (e.g. listing six different integrals without explaining sub-interval choice) create inconsistency.  Overall clarity is âmostlyâ but not fully achieved, hence band 2.

The disk method, piecewise polynomial fitting and definite integration are all on the SL course and clearly relevant to determining volume.  The mathematics chosen is appropriate but not advanced or sophisticated, matching band 3.

Photographs, GeoGebra screenshots and graphs are provided; however many are too small or blurred and several equations are separated from the corresponding graph.  The representations are present but only partly support understanding, satisfying the middle descriptor.
Reflection is confined to general statements (âmeasuring curves is trickyâ) without probing reasons or implications.  Depth is therefore limited.

Apart from importing a photo and attempting multiple functions, there is limited creativity.  No alternative strategies, error estimates or refinements are explored.  Thus only a modest level of initiative is demonstrated.

The report contains a discernible introduction, modelling section, calculation section and conclusion, so the reader can follow the overall line of thought.  However, transitions between sections are abrupt and the logical flow breaks where definitions, justification for function choices and discussion of results are missing.  Consequently the narrative is only partially convincing, matching the âsome coherence and logical developmentâ descriptor.
There is a brief acknowledgement of human error but no systematic critique of the modelling choices or numerical results (which are clearly unrealistic).  The work displays only basic analysis.
The conclusion repeats aims but does not connect findings back to mathematical understanding or evaluate accuracy.  The link to learning therefore remains superficial.

A basic three-part structure (introduction, process, conclusion) is evident and headings are used, but sub-sections are not clearly sign-posted and screenshots are inserted without captions or commentary in places.  Hence organisation is adequate but not âwell organisedâ; band 2 is appropriate.

A short narrative about helping the grandfather constitutes a personal context, yet the exploration is not driven forward by that perspective; decisions (e.g. function types, interval breaks) are not justified from a personal standpoint.  This fits âsome evidence of personal approach.â

annotations.comment: The title is clear and immediately identifies the exploration topic, aiding coherence and reader orientation.
Consider stating an explicit research question or aim on the title page to guide the readerâs expectations from the start.
The extensive anecdotal introduction detracts from mathematical focus; condense narrative and remove repetition for conciseness.
The personal story about the grandfather provides genuine motivation and shows engagement with the topic.
Figure 1 has no descriptive caption or in-text reference; add a clear caption and discuss its relevance in the introduction.
Citation in the footnote is inconsistently formatted; ensure all references follow a coherent IB citation style.
The aim statement is vague; restate it precisely (e.g. âTo model the Ollaâs profile and compute its volume via $$\int_a^b\pi[f(x)]^2\,dx$$â).
Detail the scaling process and quantify measurement uncertainty when transferring the photograph to GeoGebra.
The GeoGebra plot (Figure 2) lacks axis labels and unit markings, which reduces clarity of the model.
The description of Riemann sums is inaccurate and the term is misspelled; clarify the disk method as a particular limit of Riemann sums.
The equation $$f(x)=r$$ appears without context; define how the radius relates to the function output for clarity.
Replace the informal note âas infinite is not a numberâ with an explanation invoking limits and the fundamental theorem of calculus.
Explicitly include the differential element $$dx$$ in the summation expression header to reinforce mathematical rigour.
Outline each piecewise function and its valid interval before presenting integrals to guide the readerâs understanding.
In Figure 5 the fitted functions overlap without legend or annotations; add labels to distinguish each model clearly.
The cubic functions p(x), q(x), and g(x) are shown without justification or derivation; explain how they were fitted to the profile.
The reference â(Menz, 2018)â is not included in the bibliography; add full citation to maintain academic integrity.
Figure 8 is introduced before discussion and has no descriptive caption; reposition and caption it to improve organization.
The disk method formula $$V=\int_a^b\pi[f(x)]^2\,dx$$ is correctly applied, demonstrating solid understanding of volume integration.
The integration limits (0 to 29) conflict with an earlier measured width of 28 cm; reconcile this discrepancy or justify the choice.
Provide a concise rationale for segmenting the integration into six intervals to clarify the piecewise modelling strategy.
Define all variables and notation (e.g. x, y, f(x)) explicitly before integrating to maintain clarity and consistency.
The computed value for $$V_4$$ is implausibly large relative to the Ollaâs dimensions; re-examine the integration and algebra for errors.
Several intermediate volumes lack units in their results; append âcmÂ³â to each calculation for clarity.
The total volume sum is presented without validation; perform a sanity check against a simple bounding cylinder estimate.
The conclusion omits critical evaluation of numerical accuracy and implications of measurement error; include deeper reflection.
Reflective commentary on personal growth and IB identity adds authenticity and demonstrates engagement.
Bibliographic entries are inconsistently formatted and lack retrieval dates; standardize all references to an IBâapproved style.
Include full author names, publication dates, and URLs for all online sources to strengthen academic rigour in the bibliography.
The âBibliographyâ heading uses an H2 style; format headings consistently throughout the report.
